steps.text: Keep the other Party generally informed of its relevant plans and schedules.
Respond to the other Party's requests for information to the extent reasonable and practical.
Strive to recognize and minimize any problems arising from each Party's regulatory activities in implementing this MOU.
Consult with the NRC before the promulgation of any standards of general application.
Promulgate regulations for regulating ISR activities.

preconditions.text: Until such time as the EPA promulgates new standards of general application under AEA § 275, the existing groundwater protection standards currently set forth in 40 CFR Part 192, Subpart D must be used.

constraints.text: Apply the corrective action framework under Subtitle C of the SWDA as the model for developing standards of general application concerning or relating to those aquifers in ISR wellfields that have been restored to approved groundwater protection standards.
Do not direct the use of a particular, detailed management, engineering, or technical method or test to demonstrate licensee compliance in any standards of general application.
Do not restrict the regulatory agency's selection of an appropriate methodology or test to monitor licensee compliance with the standards in any standards of general application.

steps.text: Request the concurrence of the EPA that general requirements for the management of byproduct material are at least comparable to requirements applicable to the possession, transfer, and disposal of similar hazardous material regulated by the EPA under the SWDA.

Send the request in writing from the Director, Office of Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, to the EPA through the Director, Office of Radiation and Indoor Air, including a copy of the NRC's draft final rule.

preconditions.text: NRC rulemaking would promulgate general requirements for the management of byproduct material.

steps.text: Make all reasonable efforts to resolve the disagreement collegially and quickly, consistent with the principles described in Section VI of this agreement.
Raise resolution of the disagreement to the Office of the Executive Director for Operations for the NRC and to the Assistant Administrator for Air and Radiation for the EPA.
Endeavor to resolve all disagreements prior to publication of the final rule in the Federal Register.

preconditions.text: A disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule containing the general requirements for the management of byproduct material.
Resolution of the disagreement is necessary to be raised (i.e., it is not resolved collegially and quickly).

constraints.text: This MOU is not intended to be legally binding.
All commitments made by the EPA and the NRC in this MOU are subject to the availability of appropriated funds and budget priorities.

steps.text: Designate a representative and alternate representative, as described in Attachment A, to ensure coordination between the EPA and the NRC.
Execute this MOU on the dates shown.
Provide written notice to the other Party to change representative.
Provide written notice to the other Party at least 30 days in advance of the desired termination date to terminate participation.

preconditions.text: If a Party wishes to change its representative
If a Party wishes to terminate its participation in this MOU

constraints.text: This MOU does not apply to any person outside of the NRC and the EPA.
